design_philosophy.core_loop: Select an elemental plane from hub → traverse three platforming levels with time pressure → defeat boss → return to hub to select next plane or restart if time expires
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure (hourglass timer) forcing level completion before returning to hub; escalating difficulty across four elemental planes leading to four 'planes of evil'
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying Chakan, an immortal warrior cursed to eternally battle supernatural evil across dimensions after defeating Death itself
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Intentionally punishing design philosophy: high difficulty by design to convey 'the pain and the futility of Chakan's circular existence.' Players must repeat entire plane sequences on death rather than continue mid-level, mirroring Chakan's curse of endless repetition. Non-linear plane selection with hub-based structure creates agency in route choice but not difficulty mitigation. Game deliberately denies closure—even defeating the final boss yields only 'Not the end' message, forcing players to experience Chakan's existential futility.

difficulty.lives_system: Infinite lives, but death returns player to hub and restarts current plane from level one
difficulty.checkpointing: Plane-level checkpointing only; no mid-plane saves. Three-level structure per plane means progression loss on any death
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency in plane selection order (non-linear progression through four elemental planes), but no difficulty selection or assist modes. Weapon acquisition (grappling hook, hammer) and potion combinations provide tactical customization within levels

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition system: Chakan starts with twin swords; acquires new weapons in first stage of each elemental plane (grappling hook for climbing, hammer for breaking walls, etc.). Potion collection and combination system allows temporary buffs (elemental enhancement, jump height increase)
progression.unlock_mechanism: Completing three levels + boss of an elemental plane unlocks access to the corresponding 'plane of evil' (fire, earth, wind, water). Successfully clearing all four elemental planes of evil triggers final boss encounter
replayability_hooks: Non-linear plane selection allows different route-through difficulty progression
Potion combination system creates experimentation potential
High difficulty naturally encourages multiple playthroughs and pattern learning
Cult following suggests speedrun and sequence-optimization community engagement
Final boss is single-attempt only, creating high-stakes finale

world_building_techniques: Mythological framing: Chakan's pact with Death establishes existential stakes and curse narrative
Environmental storytelling: Dark, moody H.R. Giger-esque art direction conveys supernatural horror atmosphere across elemental planes
Thematic visual design: Parallax scrolling and pixelated art exploitation create dimensional otherworldliness
Meta-narrative design: Non-linear plane structure and hub repetition reinforce Chakan's circular, endless existence
Closure denial: 'Not the end' message and post-credits boss design narratively trap player in Chakan's curse, forcing recognition that every star contains a planet with supernatural evil

boss_design.philosophy: Bosses appear at end of each three-level plane sequence and serve as plane gatekeepers. Four elemental plane bosses, then four 'planes of evil' bosses (considerably harder), culminating in single-attempt final cosmic supernatural evil boss. Boss design tied to thematic progression through difficulty escalation
notable_bosses: Four elemental plane bosses (fire, earth, wind, water)
Four planes of evil bosses (considerably harder counterparts)
Final cosmic supernatural evil boss (single-attempt, post-credits encounter)

faction_indicators: Death as antagonistic cosmic force granting Chakan's curse
Supernatural evil entities across dimensional planes (elemental and cosmic)
danger_signals: Cursed immortality: Chakan's condition as both blessing and eternal punishment
Dimensional supernatural evil: escalating threat across four planes of evil
Cosmic horror implication: infinite planets with supernatural evil, curse never to be lifted
Time pressure mechanics: hourglass symbolism reinforces mortality threat despite immortality
environmental_storytelling: Elemental plane theming (earth, air, fire, water) with level-specific visual design
H.R. Giger-influenced dark, moody aesthetics suggest cosmic horror and bodily dread
Weapon acquisition tied to plane progression, suggesting environmental hazards and obstacles
Potion elements (fire, earth, wind, water) align with plane structure, reinforcing dimensional organization
Hub stage serves as neutral ground between supernatural dimensions
